description: Fix bugs and missing guards in the plugin SDK and plugin-tools packages

userStories.title: Add try-catch around document.cookie access in getCookie
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want getCookie to gracefully handle SecurityError in sandboxed iframes so that it returns null instead of crashing, consistent with all other storage utilities
acceptanceCriteria: getCookie wraps document.cookie access in a try-catch that returns null on SecurityError
The defensive pattern matches getLocalStorage, getSessionStorage, and the other storage functions in the same file
userStories.notes: In platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts line 83, `document.cookie.split('; ')` is not wrapped in try-catch. Every other storage function in this file (getLocalStorage, setLocalStorage, getSessionStorage, setSessionStorage, removeLocalStorage, removeSessionStorage) wraps the storage access in try-catch. Wrap the entire function body (lines 82-93) in a try-catch that returns null on any error, matching the pattern of the other functions.

userStories.title: Fix fetchJSONImpl to validate schema on 204/empty responses
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want fetchJSON with a schema to properly handle 204 responses so that the return type matches the schema's type contract instead of silently returning undefined
acceptanceCriteria: When a schema is provided and the response is 204/empty, fetchJSONImpl validates undefined against the schema (which will fail for schemas that don't accept undefined) instead of silently returning undefined
When no schema is provided, 204/empty responses still return undefined (backward compatible)
userStories.notes: In platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts lines 94-96, fetchJSONImpl returns undefined immediately on 204/content-length:0 without running the schema validation. If a schema is provided, the caller expects z.infer<T> but gets undefined. Fix by moving the early return to only apply when no schema is provided. When a schema IS provided, run safeParse(undefined) so the schema can decide whether undefined is valid. Alternatively, throw a ToolError.validation explaining the server returned no content when the schema expected data.

userStories.title: Add attribute observation to waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want waitForSelector to detect attribute-based selector matches (e.g., [data-ready='true'], .loaded) so that elements that gain matching attributes after DOM insertion are properly detected
acceptanceCriteria: waitForSelector's MutationObserver includes { attributes: true, attributeFilter: undefined } in its observe options when the selector contains attribute selectors or class selectors
waitForSelectorRemoval also observes attribute changes for the same reason
Simple tag/ID selectors continue to use childList-only observation for efficiency
userStories.notes: In platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts, both waitForSelector (line 56) and waitForSelectorRemoval (line 91) use `{ childList: true, subtree: true }` without `attributes: true`. This means selectors like `[data-loaded='true']` or `.active` won't trigger the observer when an existing element gains the matching attribute. The simplest fix is to always add `attributes: true` to the observe options. A more targeted approach is to detect if the selector contains attribute selectors (brackets) or class selectors (dots) and only add `attributes: true` in those cases. The simpler approach (always observe attributes) is slightly less efficient but more correct and less fragile.

userStories.title: Handle tsc ENOENT crash in plugin-tools build command
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want opentabs-plugin build to show a helpful error message when tsc is not installed instead of crashing with TypeError
acceptanceCriteria: When spawnSync('tsc') returns with error (ENOENT), the build command shows a clear message like 'tsc not found — run npm install to install TypeScript'
The tscResult.error property is checked before accessing tscResult.stderr/stdout
userStories.notes: In platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts lines 927-930, when tsc is not found (ENOENT), spawnSync returns { error: Error, status: null, stdout: null, stderr: null }. Line 928 calls `tscResult.stderr.toString()` which throws TypeError on null. Fix by checking `tscResult.error` first: if (tscResult.error) { if (tscResult.error.code === 'ENOENT') throw new Error('tsc not found...'); throw tscResult.error; }. Then for the existing status check, guard .toString() calls with optional chaining or nullish coalescing.
